title: compute_instance
description: One virtual machine instance on any cloud provider — AWS EC2, GCP Compute Engine, Azure VM. The single cross-vendor shape policies use for network-exposure, encryption, and monitoring checks. Providers set additionalProperties for their vendor-specific fields (vpc_id, iam_instance_profile for AWS; uses_default_service_account, shielded_vm_enabled for GCP).

id.description: Unique stable identifier (AWS: instance-id, GCP: instance name, Azure: resource ID).

name.description: Human-readable name (tag Name for AWS, instance name for GCP).

provider.description: Cloud provider short name: aws, gcp, azure.

region.description: Region or zone the instance runs in.

is_running.description: Instance is in a running/active state.

has_public_ip.description: Instance has at least one public/external IP address.

root_volume_encrypted.description: Root/boot disk is encrypted at rest.

monitoring_enabled.description: Detailed/enhanced monitoring is enabled (AWS detailed monitoring). Optional: a source that cannot determine a per-instance detailed-monitoring signal (e.g. GCP Compute Engine, where it depends on the Ops Agent) omits this field, and monitoring policies guard it with is_set and scope such instances out as a coverage gap rather than reporting a fabricated pass.

metadata_service_hardened.description: Instance metadata service is hardened (AWS: IMDSv2 required; GCP: metadata server concealment enabled).
